asset.title: Workbook Style Toggle
asset.purpose: README live walkthrough clip. Judge whether the feature is obvious, visually consistent, and credible as evidence.

judge.summary: The walkthrough clearly demonstrates toggling between different workbook style views (Excel File, Sheets Collab, and Evidence Review) within the NodeRoom interface, providing solid evidence of the feature's functionality.

featureClarity.evidence: The video clearly shows the user clicking through the 'Excel File', 'Sheets Collab', and 'Evidence' tabs to toggle the workbook style.

workflowCompleteness.evidence: Demonstrates the complete flow from opening the workbook to switching between all three available views.

visualDesign.evidence: The dark mode UI is clean, well-aligned, and the active tab states are clearly highlighted.

consistency.evidence: Matches the NodeRoom ecosystem, featuring the chat, room trace, and spreadsheet components working together.

evidenceQuality.evidence: Captured directly from the running application, showing real-time UI state changes and cell selections.

legibility.evidence: Text in the spreadsheet cells and the tab labels is legible, though some sidebar text is small.

professionalRelevance.evidence: Highly relevant for financial and collaborative workflows where data provenance and presentation styles must be toggled.

productionHonesty.evidence: Accurately depicts local UI changes without overclaiming backend synchronization behavior.

observedEvidence: Excel File mode displaying merged cells and formula bar
Sheets Collab mode showing active cell selection
Evidence Review mode highlighting cell provenance

defects.observed: The transition between the workbook views is rapid, making it slightly difficult to digest the visual differences immediately.
defects.fix: Add a brief 1-2 second pause on each view state during recording.

judge.suggestedReadmeCaption: Toggle your workbook view between raw Excel File format, collaborative Sheets mode, and verified Evidence Review mode to inspect data provenance without altering the underlying canonical sheet.
